code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test10-APR-2019Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is impaired (elevated creatinine), and urinalysis shows significant proteinuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting possible renal pathology (e.g., nephrotic syndrome, acute kidney injury). Blood glucose and HbA1c are low, indicating possible hypoglycemia or malnutrition. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further evaluation of renal function, nutritional status, and possible underlying causes for proteinuria and ketonuria.

coding.display: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
